name: Coopers Gap Wind Farm
notable: Largest wind farm in Queensland. 123 turbines (91x GE 3.6MW + 32x GE 3.8MW). AGL developed, sold to Tilt 2017.

timeline.title: EPBC Referral 2008/4237
timeline.detail: Coopers Gap Wind Farm — Not Controlled Action

timeline.title: Financial close — PARF acquisition
timeline.detail: AGL sold project to Powering Australian Renewables Fund; PPA at <$60/MWh bundled for 5 years

timeline.title: Turbine installation begins
timeline.detail: First GE turbines installed after September 2018 delivery

timeline.title: Commercial operations commenced
timeline.detail: Commencement date from AEMO registration via OpenElectricity.

timeline.title: First generation recorded
timeline.detail: First market dispatch data recorded in AEMO systems.

timeline.title: Last turbine completed
timeline.detail: All 123 GE turbines erected; commissioning continued

eis_specs.document_title: Coopers Gap Wind Farm Coordinator-General's Report (QLD DSDILGP EIS)
eis_specs.hub_height_note: Weighted average; GE 3.6-137 and GE 3.8-130 turbines at 127 m hub height
eis_specs.wind_speed_period: Mean annual wind speed at hub height; QLD South Burnett highlands — modelled from on-site met mast and BOM reference data
eis_specs.energy_yield_method: WAsP modelling with mesoscale downscaling; P50 net annual energy yield after array losses and availability
eis_specs.connection_distance_note: ~35 km of new 275 kV single-circuit line from wind farm to Powerlink Tarong substation near Nanango, QLD.
eis_specs.connection_augmentation: New 275 kV connection line to Tarong. Powerlink augmented Tarong substation bus and protection systems. QLD transmission investment assessed under RIT-T process.
eis_specs.notes: 453 MW wind farm, South Burnett region, QLD. Developer: AGL Energy (formerly Arrow Energy/APA). EPC: CATCON. Mixed GE fleet (GE 3.6-137 and GE 3.8-130 turbines). Now operating (2023). Original EIS approved 2016 by QLD Coordinator-General; turbine model updated during procurement. South Burnett highlands provide moderate-to-good wind resource for QLD conditions. EIS was assessed under the State Development and Public Works Organisation Act 1971 (QLD).
